title: Remuneration and other Facilities of Chief Minister and Minister
text: The remuneration and other facilities of the Chief Minister and Ministers shall be as provided by a State Act, and until such Act is made, shall be as specified by the State Government.

title: Oath
text: The Chief Minister and Ministers shall, before assuming their respective offices, take an oath of office and secrecy before the Chief of State, and Ministers of State and Assistant Ministers, before the Chief Minister, as provided for in the State law.

title: To give information to Chief of State
text: The Chief Minister shall inform the Chief of State about the following matters: (a) resolutions of the State Council of Ministers, (b) Bills to be introduced in the State Assembly, (c) such other necessary information as commanded by the Chief of State on matters set forth in clauses (a) and (b), and (d) current general state of affairs of the State.

title: Transaction of business of State Government
text: The allocation and transaction of business of the State Government shall be carried out in accordance with the rules approved by the State Government.

title: Transaction of business of State Government
text: No question shall be raised in any court as to whether or not the rules under clause

title: Transaction of business of State Government
text: have been observed. Part-14 State Legislature

title: State Legislature
text: The legislature of a State shall be unicameral, which shall be called as the State Assembly.

title: Composition of State Assembly
text: Members in a number that is twice as many as the number of members elected to the House of Representatives from the concerned State, through the first past the post electoral system,

title: Composition of State Assembly
text: The number of members to be set under clause

title: Composition of State Assembly
text: shall be considered to be sixty percent, and the rest forty percent members to be elected, through the proportional electoral system.

title: Composition of State Assembly
text: of clause

title: Composition of State Assembly
text: Sixty percent members of the State Assembly shall be elected in accordance with the first past the post electoral system and the forty percent members in accordance with the proportional electoral system.

title: Composition of State Assembly
text: Election to the members of the State Assembly under clause

title: Composition of State Assembly
text: shall be held through adult suffrage by secret ballots in accordance with law.

title: Composition of State Assembly
text: Each citizen of Nepal who resides within the territory of the State and who has completed the age of eighteen years shall have a right to vote in any one election constituency in accordance with law.

title: Composition of State Assembly
text: The Federal law shall provide that, in fielding candidacy by political parties for the election to the State Assembly under the proportional electoral system, representation shall be ensured on the basis of a closed list also from women, Dalit, indigenous, indigenous nationalities, Khas Arya, Madhesi, Tharu, Muslims and backward regions, minority communities, on the basis of population. In so fielding candidacy, regard shall also be had to geographical balance of the concerned State. Explanation: For the purposes of this clause, "Khas Arya" means Kshetri, Brahmin, Thakuri, Sanyasi (Dashnami) community.

title: Composition of State Assembly
text: In fielding candidacy under clause

title: Composition of State Assembly
text: , political parties must provide for representation of the persons with disabilities as well.

title: Composition of State Assembly
text: If the seat of a member of the State Assembly falls vacant while its term still remains for more than six months, the vacancy shall be filled through the same electoral system as through which such seat was filled in.

title: Composition of State Assembly
text: of clause

title: Composition of State Assembly
text: of that clause, so elect that women members constitute at least one third of the total number of members elected to the State Assembly from that party.

title: Composition of State Assembly
text: A person who is qualified under Article 178 and entitled to vote in an election to the members of the State Assembly may, subject to law, be a candidate in any election constituency of the State. Provided that a person shall not be a candidate in more than one election constituencies at the same time.

title: Composition of State Assembly
text: Other provisions relating to election to the State Assembly shall be as provided for in the Federal law.

title: Term of State Assembly
text: Unless dissolved earlier pursuant to this Constitution, the term of the State Assembly shall be five years.

title: Term of State Assembly
text: Notwithstanding anything contained in clause

title: Term of State Assembly
text: , the term of the State Assembly may be extended by a State Act for a period not exceeding one year in cases where a proclamation or order of a state of emergency is in effect.

title: Term of State Assembly
text: The term of the State Assembly extended under clause

title: Term of State Assembly
text: shall ipso facto be terminated after the expiry of six months from the date of voidance of the proclamation or order of the state of emergency in the concerned State.

title: Qualification for member of State Assembly
text: being a citizen of Nepal,

title: Qualification for member of State Assembly
text: being a voter of the concerned State,

title: Qualification for member of State Assembly
text: having completed the age of twenty five years,

title: Qualification for member of State Assembly
text: not having been convicted of a criminal offense involving moral turpitude,

title: Qualification for member of State Assembly
text: not being disqualified by any law, and

title: Qualification for member of State Assembly
text: not holding any office of profit. Explanation: For the purposes of this clause, "office of profit" means any position, other than a political position which is to be filled by election or nomination, for which a remuneration or economic benefit is paid out of a government fund.

title: Qualification for member of State Assembly
text: If a person who holds a political office to be filled by way of election, nomination or appointment is elected as a member of the State Assembly under this Part, his or her such office shall ipso facto be vacant with effect from the day on which he or she takes an oath of such office.

title: Oath of member of State Assembly
text: Every member of the State Assembly must, before taking part for the first time in the meeting of the Assembly or any of its committees, take an oath as provided for in the State law.

title: Vacation of seat of member of State Assembly
text: The seat of a member of the State Assembly shall become vacant in any of the following circumstances: (a) if he or she tenders resignation in writing before the Speaker of the State Assembly, (b) if he or she is no longer qualified or ceases to possess the qualification under Article 178, (c) if the term of the State Assembly expires or it is dissolved, (d) if he or she absents himself or herself from ten consecutive meetings, without giving notice to the State Assembly, (e) if the political party of which he or she was a member when elected provides a notification as provided for in the Federal law that he or she has defected from the party, (f) If he or she dies.

title: Decision as to disqualification of member
text: If a question arises as to whether any member of the State Assembly is disqualified or has ceased to possess any of the qualifications under Article 178, the Constitutional Bench of the Supreme Court shall make the final decision of such question.

title: Speaker and Deputy Speaker of State Assembly
text: The members of the State Assembly shall, not later than fifteen days of the date of holding of the first meeting of the State Assembly, elect a State Speaker and a Deputy State Speaker from amongst themselves.

title: Speaker and Deputy Speaker of State Assembly
text: Election under clause

title: Speaker and Deputy Speaker of State Assembly
text: shall be so held that there is one woman out of the State Speaker and the Deputy State Speaker, and the State Speaker and the Deputy State Speaker of the State Assembly shall be representatives from different parties. Provided that if there is no representation of more than one party in the State Assembly or no candidacy is filed by more than one party despite representation, nothing shall prevent the members of the same party from being the State Speaker and the Deputy State Speaker of the State Assembly.

title: Speaker and Deputy Speaker of State Assembly
text: If the office of the State Speaker or the Deputy State Speaker falls vacant, the members of the State Assembly shall fill the vacancy through election to the State Speaker or the Deputy State Speaker from amongst themselves.

title: Speaker and Deputy Speaker of State Assembly
text: The Deputy State Speaker shall, in the absence of the State Speaker of the State Assembly, chair the State Assembly.

title: Speaker and Deputy Speaker of State Assembly
text: If the election to the State Speaker and the Deputy State Speaker has not taken place, or if both the positions have become vacant, the attending member who is by age the senior-most shall chair the meeting of the State Assembly.

title: Speaker and Deputy Speaker of State Assembly
text: if he or she ceases to be a member of the State Assembly, Provided that, in the event of the dissolution of the State Assembly, the State Speaker and the Deputy State Speaker of the State Assembly holding their respective offices shall continue in office until the previous day of the filing of nominations for another election to the State Assembly,

title: Speaker and Deputy Speaker of State Assembly
text: if he or she resigns in writing,

title: Speaker and Deputy Speaker of State Assembly
text: if a resolution is passed by a majority of two-thirds of the total number of the then members of the State Assembly to the effect that his or her conduct is not compatible with his or her office.

title: Speaker and Deputy Speaker of State Assembly
text: The State Deputy Speaker shall preside over a meeting at which deliberations are to be held on a motion that the conduct of the Speaker of the State Assembly is not compatible with his or her office. The State Speaker shall be entitled to take part and vote in the deliberations on such motion.

title: Summoning and prorogation of session of State Assembly
text: The Chief of State shall summon a session of the State Assembly within twenty days of the declaration of final results of the election to the State Assembly. Thereafter, the Chief of State shall, from time to time, summon other sessions pursuant to this Constitution. Provided that the interval between the two consecutive sessions shall not exceed six months.

title: Summoning and prorogation of session of State Assembly
text: The Chief of State may prorogue the sessions of the State Assembly.

title: Summoning and prorogation of session of State Assembly
text: If, during the prorogation or recess of the session of the State Assembly, one-fourth of the total number of its members make a petition that it is expedient to convene a session or meeting, the Chief of State shall specify the date and time for such session or meeting. The State Assembly shall meet or commence its session at the date and time so specified.

title: Address by Chief of State
text: The Chief of State may address a sitting of the State Assembly, and summon the members for that purpose.

title: Address by Chief of State
text: The Chief of State shall address the first session after an election to the State Assembly and a sitting of the State Assembly after the commencement of the first session of each year.

title: Quorum of State Assembly
text: Except as otherwise provided for in this Constitution, no question or motion shall be presented for decision in the State Assembly unless one-fourth of the total number of its members are present.

title: Voting in State Assembly
text: All questions submitted for decision in the State Assembly shall be decided by a majority vote of the members present and participate in voting. The member chairing the meeting shall not have the right to vote. Provided that he or she may exercise his or her casting vote in the case of a tie.

title: Privileges of State Assembly
text: There shall be full freedom of speech in the State Assembly, subject to this Constitution, and no member shall be arrested, detained or prosecuted in any court for anything expressed or any vote cast by him or her in the State Assembly.

title: Privileges of State Assembly
text: The State Assembly shall, subject to this Constitution, have full power to regulate and decide its internal business, and it shall be the exclusive right of the State Assembly to decide whether or not any of its proceedings is regular or irregular. No question shall be raised in any court in this behalf.

title: Privileges of State Assembly
text: No comment shall be made about the good faith concerning any proceeding of the State Assembly, and no publication and broadcasting of any kind shall be made about anything said by any member, intentionally distorting or misinterpreting the meaning of the speech.

title: Privileges of State Assembly
text: The provisions of clauses

title: Privileges of State Assembly
text: and

title: Privileges of State Assembly
text: shall also apply to other persons who are entitled to participate in the meetings of the State Assembly than the members of State Assembly.

title: Privileges of State Assembly
text: No proceedings shall be initiated in any court against any person in respect of the publication, under the authority granted by the State Assembly, of any document, report, vote or proceeding. Explanation: For the purposes of this clause and clauses

title: Privileges of State Assembly
text: and

title: Privileges of State Assembly
text: , "State Assembly" means and includes the meeting of a committee of the State Assembly.

title: Privileges of State Assembly
text: No member of the State Assembly may be arrested during the period from the issuance of a notice summoning the session to its prorogation. Provided that nothing in this clause shall be deemed to prevent the arrest under the law of any member on a criminal charge. If any member is so arrested, the authority making such arrest shall forthwith give information thereof to the person presiding over the State Assembly.

title: Privileges of State Assembly
text: Any breach of the privileges shall be deemed to constitute contempt of the State Assembly, and the State Assembly shall have the exclusive right to decide whether any breach of its privileges has taken place.

title: Privileges of State Assembly
text: If any person is in contempt of the State Assembly, the person presiding over the State Assembly may, after a decision by the State Assembly to that effect, admonish, warn or impose a sentence of imprisonment for a term not exceeding three months or of a fine not exceeding ten thousand rupees on such person, and such fine shall be recovered as a government due. Provided that if such a person submits an apology to the satisfaction of the State Assembly, it may either pardon him or her or remit or commute the sentence imposed on him or her.

title: Privileges of State Assembly
text: Other matters relating to privileges of the State Assembly shall be as provided in the State law.

title: Provisions relating to vote of confidence and motion of no-confidence
text: The Chief Minister may, whenever he or she considers necessary or appropriate to show that he or she has confidence from the State Assembly, table a resolution to that effect in the State Assembly for the vote of confidence.

title: Provisions relating to vote of confidence and motion of no-confidence
text: If the political party which the Chief Minister represents is divided or a political party in coalition State Government withdraws its support, the Chief Minister shall table a resolution in the State Assembly for a vote of confidence within thirty days.

title: Provisions relating to vote of confidence and motion of no-confidence
text: If a resolution tabled under clauses

title: Provisions relating to vote of confidence and motion of no-confidence
text: and

title: Provisions relating to vote of confidence and motion of no-confidence
text: is not passed by a majority of the total number of then members of the State Assembly, the Chief Minister shall relieve of his or her office.

title: Provisions relating to vote of confidence and motion of no-confidence
text: One-fourth of the total number of then members of the State Assembly may table in writing a motion of no-confidence against the Chief Minister. Provided that a motion of no confidence may not be tabled until the first two years after the appointment of the Chief Minister and until another one year after the date of failure of the motion of no confidence once tabled.

title: Provisions relating to vote of confidence and motion of no-confidence
text: A motion of no confidence to be tabled under clause

title: Provisions relating to vote of confidence and motion of no-confidence
text: shall also indicate the name of a member proposed for Chief Minister.

title: Provisions relating to vote of confidence and motion of no-confidence
text: If a motion of no confidence tabled under clause

title: Provisions relating to vote of confidence and motion of no-confidence
text: is passed by a majority of the total number of then members of the State Assembly, the Chief Minister shall relieve of his or her office.

title: Provisions relating to vote of confidence and motion of no-confidence
text: If the office of the Chief Minister falls vacant because of the passage of a vote of no-confidence under clause

title: Provisions relating to vote of confidence and motion of no-confidence
text: , the Chief of State shall, in accordance with Article 168, appoint as the Chief Minister the member of the State Assembly proposed under clause

title: Provisions relating to vote of confidence and motion of no-confidence
Minister, Minister of State and Assistant Minister entitled to take part in meetings of State Assembly
text: The Minister, Minister of State and Assistant Minister shall be entitled to attend, and take part in the proceedings and deliberations of, the State Assembly or its committees. Provided that a Minister, Minister of State or Assistant Minister who is not a member of the State Assembly shall not be entitled to vote in a meeting of the State Assembly or its committee, and a Minister, Minister of State or Assistant Minister shall not be entitled to vote in a meeting of a committee of which he or she is not a member.

title: Penalty for unauthorized presence or voting in State Assembly
text: If a person who has not taken oath under Article 179 or who is not a member of the State Assembly is present or votes in a meeting of the State Assembly or of its committee as a member, the person shall, by order of the person chairing the meeting, be fined with a sum of five thousand rupees for each instance of such presence or voting, and such a fine shall be recovered as a government due.

title: Restriction on discussion
text: No discussion shall be held in the State Assembly on any matters that may cause adverse effect on the dispensation of justice on any cases which are sub judice in any courts of Nepal and on any judicial acts done by Judges in the course of performance of their duties.

title: Transaction of business in case of vacancy in seat of member
text: The State Assembly shall have the power to transact its business notwithstanding any vacancy in the seat of its member; and no proceedings of the State Assembly already conducted shall become invalid even if it is subsequently discovered that a person who was not so entitled took part in such proceedings.

title: Power of State Assembly to form committees
text: The State Assembly may, in accordance with its rules, form committees or special committees, as required, in order to manage its working procedures.

title: Procedures relating to conduct of business of State Assembly
text: The State Assembly shall frame rules to conduct its business, maintain order during its meetings and regulate the constitution, functions and procedures of, and other matters relating to, its committees. Until such rules are framed, the State Assembly shall regulate its procedures on its own.

title: Secretary and Secretariat of State Assembly
text: The Chief of State shall appoint the Secretary of the State Assembly on recommendation of the Speaker of State Assembly.

title: Secretary and Secretariat of State Assembly
text: There shall be a Secretariat for conducting and managing the business of the State Assembly. The establishment of such Secretariat and other matters related thereto shall be as provided for in the State law.

title: Secretary and Secretariat of State Assembly
text: The qualification, functions, duties, powers and other conditions of service of the Secretary of the State Assembly shall be as provided for in the State law.

title: Remuneration
text: The remuneration and facilities of the Speaker and the Deputy Speaker of the State Assembly shall be as provided for in the State law, and as specified by the State Government until such law is made. Part-15 State Legislative Procedures

title: Legislative powers of State Assembly
text: The legislative powers of the State Assembly shall be as contained in lists of Schedule-6, Schedule-7 and Schedule-9.

title: Procedures for introduction of Bills in State Assembly
text: A Bill may, subject to this Constitution, be introduced in the State Assembly.

title: Procedures for introduction of Bills in State Assembly
text: A Money Bill and a Bill concerning peace and security may be introduced only as a Government Bill.

title: Procedures for introduction of Bills in State Assembly
text: the imposition, collection, abolition, remission, alteration or regulation of taxes in the State,

title: Procedures for introduction of Bills in State Assembly
text: the preservation of the State Consolidated Fund or any other State Government Fund, the deposit of moneys into and the appropriation or the withdrawal of moneys from such Funds, or the reduction, increment or cancellation of appropriations or of proposed expenditures from such Funds,

title: Procedures for introduction of Bills in State Assembly
text: the regulation of matters relating to the borrowing of money or the giving of guarantee by the State Government, or any matter pertaining to the amendment of the law with respect to any financial obligations undertaken or to be undertaken by the State Government,

title: Procedures for introduction of Bills in State Assembly
text: the custody and investment of all revenues received by any State Government Fund, moneys acquired through the repayment of loans, and grant moneys; or audits of the accounts of the State Government, or

title: Procedures for introduction of Bills in State Assembly

title: Procedures for introduction of Bills in State Assembly
text: . Provided that any Bill shall not be deemed to be a Money Bill by the reason only that it provides for the levying of any charges or fees such as license fee, application fee, renewal fee or for the imposition of fines or penalty of imprisonment.

title: Procedures for introduction of Bills in State Assembly
text: If any question arises whether a Bill is a Money Bill or not, the decision of the Speaker of State Assembly thereon shall be final.

title: Procedures for passage of Bills
text: A Bill passed by the State Assembly shall be presented to the Chief of State for assent.

title: Procedures for passage of Bills
text: If a session of the State Assembly terminates while a Bill is under consideration, deliberations on the Bill may continue at the succeeding session. Provided that if the State Assembly is dissolved or its term expires when any Bill is under its consideration, such a Bill shall lapse.

title: Withdrawal of Bills
text: A member who has introduced a Bill may, with the approval of the State Assembly, withdraw the Bill.

title: Assent on Bills
text: A Bill which is to be presented to the Chief of State for assent under Article 199 shall be so presented by the Speaker of State Assembly after it has been certified by him or her. Provided that in the case of a Money Bill, the Speaker of State Assembly shall so certify.

title: Assent on Bills
text: A Bill presented to the Chief of State for his or her assent shall be assented to within fifteen days, and the State Assembly shall be informed thereof as soon as possible.

title: Assent on Bills
text: Except for a Money Bill, if the Chief of State is of the opinion that any Bill needs further deliberations, he or she may send back the Bill with his or her message to the State Assembly within fifteen days from the date of presentation of the Bill to him or her.

title: Assent on Bills
text: If any Bill is sent back with his or her message by the Chief of State under clause

title: Assent on Bills
text: , it shall be reconsidered by the State Assembly and if the Bill so reconsidered is again passed as it was or with amendments, and is again presented to him or her, the Chief of State shall give assent to that Bill within fifteen days of such presentation.

title: Assent on Bills
text: A Bill shall become an Act after the Chief of State gives assent to it.

title: Ordinance
text: If, at any time, except when the State Assembly is in session, a circumstance exists which renders it necessary to take immediate action, the Chief of State may, on the recommendation of the State Council of Ministers, promulgate an Ordinance.

title: Ordinance
text: An Ordinance promulgated under clause

title: Ordinance
text: shall be tabled at the session of the State Assembly held after the promulgation, and if not passed by the State Assembly, it shall ipso facto cease to be effective,

title: Ordinance
text: may be repealed at any time by the Chief of State, and

title: Ordinance
text: shall, unless rendered ineffective or repealed under sub- clause

title: Ordinance
text: or

title: Ordinance
text: , ipso facto cease to be effective at the expiration of sixty days after the day on which a meeting of the State Assembly is held. Part -16 State Financial Procedures

title: No tax to be levied or loan to be raised
text: No tax shall be levied and collected in a State except in accordance with law.

title: No tax to be levied or loan to be raised
text: No loan shall be raised and guarantee given by the State Government except as provided for in the Federal law.

title: State Consolidated Fund
text: Except for the revenues of trusts (Guthi), all revenues received by the State Government, all loans raised on the security of revenues, all moneys received in repayment of any loans made under the authority of any State Act and any mounts of grants or loans received from the Government of Nepal shall, except as otherwise provided for in the State Act, be credited to a State Government Fund to be known as the State Consolidated Fund.

title: Expenditures from State Consolidated Fund or State Government Fund
text: No expenditure shall be incurred out of the State Consolidated Fund or any other State Government Fund except the following: (a) moneys charged on the State Consolidated Fund, (b) moneys required to meet the expenditure under an Appropriation Act, (c) advance moneys authorized by an Act required to meet expenditures, when an Appropriation Bill is under consideration, or (d) expenditures to be incurred in extraordinary circumstances under a Vote of Credit Act which contains only a description of expenditures. Provided that matters relating to the State Contingency Fund shall be in accordance with Article 212.

title: Expenditures chargeable on State Consolidated Fund
text: The expenditures relating to the following matters shall be charged on the State Consolidated Fund, and approval of the State Assembly shall not be required for such expenditures: (a) the amount required as remuneration and facilities payable to the State Speaker and the State Deputy Speaker, (b) the amount required as remuneration and facilities payable to the Chairperson and members of the State Public Service Commission, (c) all charges relating to debts for which the State Government is liable, (d) any sum required to satisfy any judgment or decree made by a court against the State Government, and (e) any other sum specified by a State law to be chargeable on the State Consolidated Fund.

title: Estimates of revenues and expenditures
text: an estimate of revenues,

title: Estimates of revenues and expenditures
text: the moneys required to meet the charges on the State Consolidated Fund, and

title: Estimates of revenues and expenditures
text: the moneys required to meet the expenditure to be provided for by a State Appropriation Act.

title: Estimates of revenues and expenditures
text: The annual estimate to be laid pursuant to clause

title: Estimates of revenues and expenditures
text: shall also be accompanied by a statement of the expenses allocated to every Ministry in the previous financial year and particulars of whether the objectives of the expenses have been achieved.

title: State Appropriation Act
text: The moneys required to meet the expenditure to be provided for by any State Appropriation Act shall be specified under appropriate heads in an Appropriation Bill.

title: Supplementary estimates
text: that the sum authorized to be spent for a particular service by the State Appropriation Act for the current financial year is insufficient, or that a need has arisen for expenditures upon some new service not provided for in the State Appropriation Act for that year, or

title: Supplementary estimates
text: that the expenditures made during that financial year are in excess of the amount authorized by the State Appropriation Act.

title: Supplementary estimates
text: The sums included in the supplementary estimates shall be specified under the heads in a Supplementary Appropriation Bill.

title: Votes on Account
text: Notwithstanding anything contained elsewhere in this Part, a portion of the expenditure estimated for the financial year may, when a State Appropriation Bill is under consideration, be incurred in advance under a State Act.

title: Votes on Account
text: A Vote on Account Bill shall not be introduced until the estimates of revenues and expenditures have been laid in accordance with Article 207, and the sums involved in the Vote on Account shall not exceed one-third of the estimate of expenditures for the financial year.

title: Votes on Account
text: The expenditures incurred in accordance with the State Vote on Account Act shall be included in the State Appropriation Bill.

title: Votes of Credit
text: of Article 207, the State Minister for Finance may lay before the State Assembly a Vote of Credit Bill giving only a statement of expenditures.

title: State Contingency Fund
text: A State Act may create a Fund to be known as the State Contingency Fund into which shall be paid from time to time such moneys as may be determined by the State Act.

title: State Contingency Fund
text: The Fund under clause

title: State Contingency Fund
text: shall be under the control of the State Government. Any unforeseen expenditure may be met out of such Fund by the State Government.

title: State Contingency Fund
text: The amount of the expenditure under clause

title: State Contingency Fund
text: shall be reimbursed as soon as possible by the State Act.

title: Act relating to financial procedures
text: Matters relating to the transfer of moneys appropriated by the State Act from one head to another and other financial procedures shall be as provided for in the State Act. Part-17 Local Executive

title: Executive power of Local Level
text: The executive power of the Local Level shall, pursuant to this Constitution and the Federal law, be vested in the Village Executive or the Municipal Executive.

title: Executive power of Local Level
text: The local executive power shall be as mentioned in Schedule-8 and Schedule-9.

title: Executive power of Local Level
text: The responsibility for issuing general directives, controlling and regulating the governance of the Village Body and the Municipality shall, subject to this Constitution and other laws, lie in the Village Executive and the Municipal Executive.

title: Executive power of Local Level
text: The executive functions of the Village Body and the Municipality shall be performed in the name of the Village Executive and the Municipal Executive.

title: Executive power of Local Level
text: Any decision or order to be issued in the name of the Village Executive and the Municipal Executive pursuant to clause

title: Executive power of Local Level
text: and other instruments of authorization pertaining thereto shall be authenticated as provided for in the Local law.

title: Provisions relating to Chairperson and Vice-Chairperson of Village Executive
text: There shall be a Chairperson of Village Executive in each Village Body. The Village Executive shall be formed under his or her chairpersonship.

Provisions relating to Chairperson and Vice-Chairperson of Village Executive
text: The Chairperson and the Vice-Chairperson shall be elected by the voters residing within the concerned Village Body area by secret ballots on the basis of one person one vote, in accordance with the first past the post electoral system. Explanation: For the purposes of this Article, "Chairperson" and "Vice- Chairperson" mean the Chairperson and the Vice-Chairperson of the Village Executive.

text: A person who has been elected as the Chairperson for two terms shall not be eligible to be a candidate in an election to the Village Body.

title: Provisions relating to Mayor and Deputy Mayor of Municipal Executive
text: Each Municipality shall have a Mayor. The Municipal Executive shall be formed under his or her chairpersonship.

Provisions relating to Mayor and Deputy Mayor of Municipal Executive
text: The Mayor and the Deputy Mayor shall be elected by the voters residing within the concerned Municipal area by secret ballots on the basis of one person one vote, in accordance with the first past the post electoral system. Explanation: For the purposes of this Article, "Mayor" and "Deputy Mayor" mean the Mayor and the Deputy Mayor of the Municipal Executive.

title: Judicial Committee
text: There shall be a three-member judicial committee to be coordinated by its Vice-Chairperson in the case of a Village Body and by its Deputy Mayor in the case of a Municipality, in order to settle disputes under their respective jurisdictions in accordance with law.

text: The judicial committee under clause

title: Judicial Committee

title: Conduct of business of Village Executive and Municipal Executive
text: Allocation and conduct of business of the Village Executive and the Municipal Executive shall be carried out in accordance with the rules approved by the Village Executive and the Municipal Executive, respectively.

title: Other provisions relating to Local Level Executive
text: Other provisions relating to the Local Level Executive, except those contained in this Part, shall be as provided for in the Federal law, subject to this Constitution.

title: District Assembly and District Coordination Committee
text: There shall be a District Assembly to make coordination between the Village Bodies and Municipalities within the district.

title: District Assembly and District Coordination Committee
text: The District Assembly shall consist of Chairpersons and Vice- Chairpersons of Village Executives, and Mayors and Deputy Mayors of Municipal Executives within the district. The first meeting of the District Assembly shall be held no later than thirty days of the date of final results of the election to the Village Assemblies and the Municipal Assemblies.

title: District Assembly and District Coordination Committee
text: The District Assembly shall elect the District Coordination Committee consisting of a maximum of nine Members including one Chief, one Deputy Chief, at least three women and at least one Dalit or minority. The District Coordination Committee shall discharge all functions required to be discharged by the District Assembly.

title: District Assembly and District Coordination Committee
text: A Member of a Village Assembly or Municipal Assembly within the concerned district shall be eligible to be a candidate for the office of Chief, Deputy Chief or Member of the District Coordination Committee. If elected to the office of Chief, Deputy Chief or Member of the District Coordination Committee, his or her office of Member of Village Assembly or of Municipal Assembly shall ipso facto lapse.

title: District Assembly and District Coordination Committee
text: The term of office of the Chief, Deputy Chief and member of the District Coordination Committee shall be five years after the date of being elected.

title: Legislative powers of Local Level
text: The legislative powers of the Local Level shall be vested in the Village Assembly and the Municipal Assembly, subject to this Constitution.

title: Composition of Municipal Assembly
text: shall consist of the Mayor and the Deputy Mayor of the Municipal Executive, Ward Chairpersons, and four members elected from each Ward and members of the Municipal Executive elected from Dalit or minority communities pursuant to clause

title: Composition of Municipal Assembly
text: of Article 216.

title: Composition of Municipal Assembly
text: A Municipal Assembly to be formed under clause

title: Composition of Municipal Assembly
text: shall have representation of at least two women from each Ward.

title: Composition of Municipal Assembly
text: Each ward of a Municipality shall have a Ward Committee composed of one Ward Chairperson and four members as provided for in the Federal law. Such Ward Chairperson and Ward members shall be elected in accordance with the first past the post electoral system.

title: Composition of Municipal Assembly
text: Every person who has completed the age of eighteen years and whose name is included in the electoral rolls of the Municipality shall have a right to vote as provided for in the Federal law.

title: Composition of Municipal Assembly
text: being a citizen of Nepal,

title: Composition of Municipal Assembly
text: having completed the age of twenty one years,

title: Composition of Municipal Assembly
text: being his or her name included in the electoral rolls of the Municipality, and

title: Composition of Municipal Assembly
text: not being disqualified by any law.

title: Composition of Municipal Assembly
text: Matters relating to the election to the Municipal Assembly and other matters pertaining thereto shall be as provided for in the Federal law.

title: Chairperson and Vice-Chairperson of Village Assembly and Municipal Assembly
text: The Chairperson and Vice-Chairperson of a Village Executive and the Mayor and Deputy Mayor of a Municipal Executive shall ipso facto be the Chairperson and Vice-Chairperson of the Village Assembly and the Municipal Assembly, respectively, and perform functions as such.

title: Term of Village Assembly and Municipal Assembly
text: The term of a Village Assembly and of a Municipal Assembly shall be five years for the date of election. Another Village Assembly and Municipal Assembly shall be elected not later than six months of the expiration of such a term.

title: Powers to make law
text: A Village Assembly and a Municipal Assembly may make necessary laws on the matters set forth in the Lists contained in Schedule-8 and Schedule-9.

title: Powers to make law
text: The process for making laws under clause

title: Powers to make law
text: shall be as provided for in the State Law.

title: Other provisions relating to Village Assembly and Municipal Assembly
text: Other matters relating to the conduct of business of a Village Assembly and Municipal Assembly, rules of procedures of meetings, formation of committees, conditions in which the office of member falls vacant, facilities receivable by members of the Village Assembly and Municipal Assembly and employees and offices of the Village Body and Municipality shall be as provided for in the State law. Part-19 Local Financial Procedures

title: No tax to be levied or loan to be raised
text: No tax shall be levied and collected and no loan raised in the Local level except in accordance with law.

title: No tax to be levied or loan to be raised
text: The Local level may levy tax by law on matters falling within its domain without prejudice to national economic policies, carriage of goods and services, capital and labour market, and the neighbouring State or Local level.

title: Local Consolidated Fund
text: Each Village Body and Municipality under the Local Level shall have one Local Consolidate Fund. All revenues received by the Village Body or Municipality, any amounts of grants received from the Government of Nepal and the State Government, all loans raised by the Village Body and Municipality, and amounts received from other sources shall be credited to such a Fund.

title: Local Consolidated Fund
text: Matters relating to expenditures from the Local Consolidated Fund under clause

title: Local Consolidated Fund
text: shall be as provided for in the Local law.

title: Estimates of revenues and expenditures of Village Body and Municipality
text: The Village Executive and the Municipal Executive shall, subject to this Constitution, lay an estimate of revenues and expenditures of every financial year before, and have the estimate passed by, the Village Assembly and the Municipal Assembly, respectively, as provided for in the Local law.

title: Estimates of revenues and expenditures of Village Body and Municipality
text: If, in laying an estimate of revenues and expenditures under clause

title: Estimates of revenues and expenditures of Village Body and Municipality
text: , the Village Executive or Municipal Executive is required to make a deficit budget, it must purpose also the sources to meet the deficit as provided for in the Federal law and the State law. Part-20 Interrelations between Federation, State and Local Level

title: Legislative interrelations between Federation and States
text: The Federal Law may be made to be applicable to the whole of, or, if required, to any part of, the territory of Nepal.

title: Legislative interrelations between Federation and States
text: A State law may be made to be applicable to the whole of, or as required, to any part of the territory of the State.

title: Legislative interrelations between Federation and States
text: If two or more States make a request to the Government of Nepal to make laws on any matter enumerated in Schedule-6, the Federal Parliament may make necessary laws. Such laws shall be applicable only to the concerned States.

title: Relations between Federation, State and Local level
text: The relations between the Federation, States, and Local level shall be based on the principles of cooperation, co-existence and coordination.

title: Relations between Federation, State and Local level
text: The Government of Nepal may, pursuant to this Constitution and the Federal Law, give necessary directions to any State Council of Ministers on matters of national importance and on matters to be coordinated between the States, and it shall be the duty of the concerned State Council of Ministers to abide by such directions.

title: Relations between Federation, State and Local level
text: If any such type of act as may seriously undermine the sovereignty, territorial integrity, nationality or independence of Nepal is carried out in any State, the President may, as required, warn such State Council of Ministers, suspend or dissolve the State Council of Ministers and the State Assembly for a period not exceeding six months.

title: Relations between Federation, State and Local level
text: Any suspension or dissolution of any State Council of Ministers and State Assembly made pursuant to clause

title: Relations between Federation, State and Local level
text: must get ratified by a two- thirds majority of the total number of the then members of the Federal Parliament within thirty five days.

title: Relations between Federation, State and Local level
text: If dissolution made pursuant to clause

title: Relations between Federation, State and Local level
text: is ratified by the Federal Parliament, election to the State Assembly shall be held in such State within six months. Provided that such suspension or dissolution shall ipso facto be invalid if it is not ratified by the Federal Parliament.

title: Relations between Federation, State and Local level
text: The Federal ruling shall apply to such State during the period of such suspension if the suspension made pursuant to clause

title: Relations between Federation, State and Local level
text: is ratified pursuant to clause

title: Relations between Federation, State and Local level
text: and until election to the State Assembly is held pursuant to clause

title: Relations between Federation, State and Local level
Relations between Federation, State and Local level
text: During the continuance of the Federal rule, the Federal Parliament may make laws with respect to any matter enumerated in the List contained in Schedule-6. Such laws shall continue to exist until repealed by other laws made by the concerned State Assembly.

title: Relations between Federation, State and Local level
text: The Government of Nepal may, directly or through the State Government, render necessary assistance to, and give necessary directives to, any Village Executive or Municipal Executive, pursuant to this Constitution and the Federal law. It shall be the duty of the Village Executive or Municipal Executive to abide by such directives.

title: Relations between States
text: One State shall render assistance in the execution of legal provisions or judicial and administrative decisions or orders of another State.

title: Relations between States
text: A State may exchange information and consult with another State on matters of common concern and interest, coordinate each other on their activities and legislations and extend mutual assistance.

title: Relations between States
text: A State shall, in accordance with its State law, provide equal security, treatment and facility to residents of another State.

title: Inter-State Council
text: Prime Minister - Chairperson

title: Inter-State Council
text: Minister for Home Affairs of the Government of Nepal - Member

title: Inter-State Council
text: Minister for Finance of the Government of Nepal - Member

title: Inter-State Council
text: Chief Ministers of the concerned States - Member

title: Inter-State Council
text: The Inter-State Council may meet as required.

title: Inter-State Council
text: The Inter-State Council may invite to its meeting a Minister of the Government of Nepal and a Minister of the concerned State who is responsible for the matter of dispute and a concerned expert.

title: Inter-State Council
text: The rules of procedures of the meeting of the Inter-State Council shall be as determined by the Council itself.

title: Coordination between Federation, State and Local Level
text: The Federal Parliament shall make necessary laws in order to maintain coordination between the Federation, State and Local level.

title: Coordination between Federation, State and Local Level
text: The State Assembly may maintain coordination between the State and Village Bodies or Municipalities and settle political disputes, if any, that have arisen, in coordination with the concerned Village Body, Municipality and the District Coordination Committee.

title: Coordination between Federation, State and Local Level
text: The processes and procedures for the settlement of disputes under clause

title: Coordination between Federation, State and Local Level
text: shall be as provided for in the State law.

title: Inter-State trade
text: Notwithstanding anything contained elsewhere in this Constitution, it is prohibited to make any kind of obstruction to the carriage of goods or extension of services by a State or Local level to another State or Local level or to the carriage of goods or extension of services to any State or Local Level or to levy tax, fee or charge thereon or to make any kind of discrimination on the carriage or extension of such services or goods.

title: Not to affect jurisdiction of Constitutional Bench of Supreme Court
text: Nothing contained in this Part shall affect the jurisdiction of the Constitutional Bench of the Supreme Court under Article 137. Part-21 Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority

title: Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority
text: There shall be a Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority of Nepal, consisting of the Chief Commissioner and four other Commissioners. The Chief Commissioner shall act as the Chairperson of the Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority.

title: Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority
text: The President shall, on recommendation of the Constitutional Council, appoint the Chief Commissioner and Commissioners.

title: Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority
text: The term of office of the Chief Commissioner and Commissioners shall be six years from the date of appointment.

title: Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority
text: Notwithstanding anything contained in clause

title: Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority
text: if he or she tenders resignation in writing to the President,

title: Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority
text: if he or she attains the age of sixty-five years,

title: Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority
text: if a motion of impeachment is passed against him or her under Article 101,

title: Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority
text: if he or she is removed from office by the President on recommendation of the Constitutional Council on grounds of his or her inability to hold office and discharge the functions due to physical or mental illness,

title: Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority
text: if he or she dies.

title: Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority
text: The Chief Commissioner and Commissioners appointed under clause

title: Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority
text: shall not be eligible for reappointment. Provided that a Commissioner may be appointed to the office of Chief Commissioner, and when a Commissioner is so appointed as the Chief Commissioner, his or her term of office shall be so computed as to include his or her term as the Commissioner.

title: Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority
text: holding a bachelor's degree from a recognized university,

title: Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority
text: not being a member of any political party at the time of appointment,

title: Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority
text: having gained at least twenty years of experiences in the field of either accounting, revenue, engineering, law, development or research and being a distinguished person,

title: Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority
text: having attained the age of forty five years, and

title: Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority
text: being of high moral character.

title: Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority
text: The remuneration and other conditions of service of the Chief Commissioner and the Commissioners shall be as provided for in the Federal law. The remuneration and conditions of service of the Chief Commissioner and the Commissioners shall not, so long as they hold office, be altered to their disadvantage. Provided that this provision shall not apply in cases where a state of emergency is declared because of extreme economic disarrays.

title: Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority
text: A person once appointed as the Chief Commissioner or Commissioner shall not be eligible for appointment in other government service. Provided that nothing in this clause shall be deemed to be a bar to the appointment to any political position or to any position which has the responsibility of making investigations, inquiries or findings on any subject, or to any position which has the responsibility of submitting advice, opinion or recommendation after carrying out a study or research on any subject.

title: Functions, duties and powers of the Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority
text: The Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority may, in accordance with law, conduct, or cause to be conducted, investigations of any abuse of authority committed through corruption by any person holding public office. Provided that this clause shall not apply to any official in relation to whom this Constitution itself separately provides for such action and to any official in relation to whom other law provides for a separate special provision.

title: Functions, duties and powers of the Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority
text: In the case of the persons who can be removed from office by passing a motion of impeachment under Article 101, the Judges who can be removed by the Judicial Council and the persons who are liable to action under the Army Act, it may conduct, or cause to be conducted, investigations in accordance with the Federal law after they have been removed from office.

title: Functions, duties and powers of the Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority
text: If the Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority finds, on investigation conducted pursuant to clause

title: Functions, duties and powers of the Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority
text: or

title: Functions, duties and powers of the Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority
text: , that a person holding public office has committed an act which is defined by law as corruption, it may file, or cause to be filed, a case against that person and other person involved in that offense in the competent court in accordance with law.

title: Functions, duties and powers of the Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority
text: If, on investigation conducted pursuant to clause

title: Functions, duties and powers of the Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority
text: or

title: Functions, duties and powers of the Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority
text: , any act or action done or taken by a person holding public office appears to be of such nature as to be falling under the jurisdiction of another official or body, the Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority may write to the concerned official or body for necessary action.

title: Functions, duties and powers of the Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority
text: The Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority may delegate any of its functions, duties and powers on the conducting of investigations or filing cases, to the Chief Commissioner, a Commissioner or an officer employee of the Government of Nepal to be exercised and complied with subject to the specified conditions.

title: Functions, duties and powers of the Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority
text: Other functions, duties and powers and rules of procedure of the Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority shall be as provided for in the Federal law. Part-22 Auditor General

title: Auditor General
text: There shall be an Auditor General of Nepal.

title: Auditor General
text: The President shall, on the recommendation of the Constitutional Council, appoint the Auditor General.

title: Auditor General
text: The term of office of the Auditor General shall be six years from the date of appointment.

title: Auditor General
text: Notwithstanding anything contained in clause

title: Auditor General
text: if he or she tenders resignation in writing to the President,

title: Auditor General
text: if he or she attains the age of sixty-five years,

title: Auditor General
text: if a motion of impeachment is passed against him or her under Article 101,

title: Auditor General
text: if he or she is removed from office by the President on recommendation of the Constitutional Council on grounds of his or her inability to hold office and discharge the functions due to physical or mental illness,

title: Auditor General
text: if he or she dies.

title: Auditor General
text: The Auditor General appointed under clause

title: Auditor General
text: shall not be eligible for reappointment.

title: Auditor General
text: having served in the Special Class of the Government of Nepal or having at least twenty years of experiences in audit related work, after having obtained a bachelor's degree in management, commerce or accounting from a recognized university or having passed a chartered accountancy examination,

title: Auditor General
text: not being a member of any political party at the time of appointment,

title: Auditor General
text: having attained the age of forty five years, and

title: Auditor General
text: being of high moral character.

title: Auditor General
text: The remuneration and conditions of service of the Auditor General shall be as provided for in the Federal law. The remuneration and conditions of service of the Auditor General shall not, so long as he or she holds office, be altered to his or her disadvantage. Provided that this provision shall not apply in cases where a state of emergency is declared because of extreme economic disarrays.

title: Auditor General
text: A person once appointed as the Auditor General shall not be eligible for appointment in another government service. Provided that nothing in this clause shall be deemed to be a bar to the appointment to any political position or to any position which has the responsibility of making investigations, inquiries or findings on any subject, or to any position which has the responsibility of submitting advice, opinion or recommendation after carrying out a study or research on any subject.

title: Functions, duties and powers of Auditor-General
text: The accounts of all Federal and State Government Offices including the Office of the President, Office of the Vice-President, Supreme Court, Federal Parliament, State Assembly, State Government, Local level, Constitutional Bodies and Offices thereof, Courts, Office of the Attorney General, Nepal Army, Nepal Police and Armed Police Force, Nepal shall be audited by the Auditor-General in accordance with law, having regard to, inter alia, the regularity, economy, efficiency, effectiveness and the propriety thereof.

title: Functions, duties and powers of Auditor-General
text: The Auditor General shall be consulted in the matter of appointment of an auditor to carry out the audit of a corporate body of which the Government of Nepal or State Government owns more than fifty percent of the shares or assets. The Auditor General may also issue necessary directives setting forth the principles for carrying out the audit of such corporate body.

title: Functions, duties and powers of Auditor-General
text: The Auditor General shall, at all times, have power to examine any books of accounts for the purpose of carrying out the functions under clause

title: Functions, duties and powers of Auditor-General
text: . It shall be the duty of the concerned chief of office to provide all such documents and information as may be demanded by the Auditor General or any of his or her employees.

title: Functions, duties and powers of Auditor-General
text: The accounts to be audited pursuant to clause

title: Functions, duties and powers of Auditor-General
text: shall be maintained in the form prescribed by the Auditor General, as provided for in the Federal law.

title: Functions, duties and powers of Auditor-General
text: In addition to the accounts of the offices mentioned in clause

title: Functions, duties and powers of Auditor-General
text: , the Federal law may also require the accounts of any other offices or bodies to be audited by the Auditor General. Part -23 Public Service Commission

title: Public Service Commission
text: There shall be a Public Service Commission of Nepal, consisting of the Chairperson and four other Members.

title: Public Service Commission
text: The President shall, on the recommendation of the Constitutional Council, appoint the Chairperson and Members.

title: Public Service Commission
text: At least fifty percent of the total number of Members of the Public Service Commission shall be appointed from amongst the persons who have worked for twenty or more in any government service, and the rest of the Members shall be appointed from amongst the persons who hold reputation after having done research, investigation, teaching or any other significant work in the field of science, technology, art, literature, law, public administration, sociology or other sphere of national life.

title: Public Service Commission
text: The term of office of the Chairperson and Members of the Public Service Commission shall be six years from the date of appointment.

title: Public Service Commission
text: The Chairperson and Members appointed under clause

title: Public Service Commission
text: shall not be eligible for reappointment. Provided that a Member may be appointed to the office of Chairperson, and when a Member is so appointed as the Chairperson, his or her term of office shall be so computed as to include his or her term as the Member.

title: Public Service Commission
text: Notwithstanding anything contained in clause

title: Public Service Commission
text: if he or she tenders resignation in writing to the President,

title: Public Service Commission
text: if he or she attains the age of sixty-five years,

title: Public Service Commission
text: if a motion of impeachment is passed against him or her under Article 101,

title: Public Service Commission
text: if he or she is removed from office by the President on recommendation of the Constitutional Council on grounds of his or her inability to hold office and discharge the functions due to physical or mental illness,

title: Public Service Commission

title: Public Service Commission
text: holding a master's degree from a recognized university,

title: Public Service Commission
text: not being a member of any political party at the time of appointment,

title: Public Service Commission

title: Public Service Commission
text: being of high moral character.

title: Public Service Commission
text: The remuneration and other conditions of service of the Chairperson and the members of the Public Service Commission shall be as provided for in the Federal law. The remuneration and conditions of service of the Chairperson and the Members of the Public Service Commission shall not, so long as they hold office, be altered to their disadvantage. Provided that this provision shall not apply in cases where a state of emergency is declared because of extreme economic disarrays.

title: Public Service Commission
text: A person once appointed as the Chairperson and the members of the Public Service Commission shall not be eligible for appointment in other government service. Provided that nothing in this clause shall be deemed to be a bar to the appointment to any political position or to any position which has the responsibility of making investigations, inquiries or findings on any subject, or to any position which has the responsibility of submitting advice, opinion or recommendation after carrying out a study or research on any subject.

title: Functions, duties and powers of the Public Service Commission
text: It shall be the duty of the Public Service Commission to conduct examinations for the selection of suitable candidates to be appointed to the positions in the Civil Service. Explanation: For the purposes of this Article, "positions in the Civil Service" means all positions in the services of the Government of Nepal, other than the positions in the services of Army personnel, Nepal Police or Armed Police Force, Nepal and such other positions in the services as are excluded by an Act from the Civil Service.

title: Functions, duties and powers of the Public Service Commission
text: The Public Service Commission shall conduct written examinations to be given for appointment to the offices of the Nepal Army, Nepal Police, Armed Police Force, Nepal, other Federal governmental services and of bodies corporate, other than the positions in the Civil Service. Explanation: For the purposes of this Article, "body corporate" means a corporation, company, bank or board of which more than fifty percent or more of the shares or assets is owned or controlled by the Government of Nepal, except a university or Education Service Commission, or a commission, corporation, authority, body, academy, board, centre, council and other body corporate of similar nature established under the Federal law or formed by the Government of Nepal.

title: Functions, duties and powers of the Public Service Commission
text: The Public Service Commission shall be consulted on the general principles to be followed in the course of making appointment and promotion to any position of the Nepal army, Nepal Police, Armed Police Force, Nepal and other Federal government services.

title: Functions, duties and powers of the Public Service Commission
text: The Public Service Commission shall be consulted on the laws relating to the conditions of service of the employees in the service of a body corporate and on the general principles to be followed in the course of making promotion to any position of such service and taking departmental action against any such employee.

title: Functions, duties and powers of the Public Service Commission
text: No permanent appointment to any pensionable position chargeable on the Government of Nepal shall be made except in consultation with the Public Service Commission.

title: Functions, duties and powers of the Public Service Commission
text: matters concerning the law relating to the conditions of service of the Federal Civil Service;

title: Functions, duties and powers of the Public Service Commission
text: the principles to be followed in making appointments to, promotions to, the Federal Civil Service or positions thereof and taking departmental action;

title: Functions, duties and powers of the Public Service Commission
text: matters concerning the suitability of any candidate for appointment to the Federal Civil Service position for a period of more than six months;

title: Functions, duties and powers of the Public Service Commission
text: matters concerning the suitability of any candidate for transfer or promotion from a position in one type of Federal Civil Service to a position in other type of Federal Civil Service or transfer or promotion from other government service to the Federal Civil Service or for a change in service or transfer from a position in a State Civil Service to a position in the Federal Civil Service or from a position in the Federal Civil Service to a State Civil Service,

title: Functions, duties and powers of the Public Service Commission
text: matters concerning the permanent transfer or promotion of an employee working in any position which does not require consultation with the Public Service Commission to any position which requires consultation with the Public Service Commission; and

title: Functions, duties and powers of the Public Service Commission
text: matters relating to departmental action against any employee of the Federal Civil Service.

title: Functions, duties and powers of the Public Service Commission
text: Notwithstanding anything contained in clause

title: Functions, duties and powers of the Public Service Commission
text: , matters falling within the jurisdiction of the Judicial Service Commission under Article 154 shall be governed by that Article.

title: Functions, duties and powers of the Public Service Commission
text: The Public Service Commission may so delegate any of its functions, duties and powers to its Chairperson or Member, an employee of the Government of Nepal as to be exercised and complied with subject to the specified conditions.

title: Functions, duties and powers of the Public Service Commission
text: Other functions, duties and powers and rules of procedure of the Public Service Commission shall be as provided for in the Federal law.

title: Provisions relating to State Public Service Commission
text: Each State shall have a State Public Service Commission.

title: Provisions relating to State Public Service Commission
text: The functions, duties and powers of the State Public Service Commission shall be as provided for in the State law.

title: Provisions relating to State Public Service Commission
text: The Federal Parliament shall by law determine grounds and standards for the purposes of clause

title: Provisions relating to State Public Service Commission
text: . Part-24 Election Commission

title: Election Commission

Provided that a Commissioner may be appointed to the office of Chief Election Commissioner, and when a Commissioner is so appointed as the Chief Election Commissioner, his or her term of office shall be so computed as to include his or her term as the Commissioner.

The remuneration and conditions of service of the Chief Election Commissioner and the Commissioners shall not, so long as they hold office, be altered to their disadvantage. Provided that this provision shall not apply in cases where a state of emergency is declared because of extreme economic disarrays.

title: Election Commission
text: A person once appointed as the Chief Election Commissioner and a Commissioner of the Election Commission shall not be eligible for appointment in other government service. Provided that nothing in this clause shall be deemed to be a bar to the appointment to any political position or to any position which has the responsibility of making investigations, inquiries or findings on any subject, or to any position which has the responsibility of submitting advice, opinion or recommendation after carrying out a study or research on any subject.

title: Functions, duties and powers of the Election Commission
text: The Election Commission shall, subject to this Constitution and the Federal law, conduct, supervise, direct and control the election to the President, Vice-President, members of the Federal Parliament, members of State Assemblies and members of Local level. For these purposes, the Election Commission shall prepare electoral rolls.

title: Functions, duties and powers of the Election Commission
text: The Election Commission shall hold a referendum on a matter of national importance pursuant to this Constitution and the Federal law

title: Functions, duties and powers of the Election Commission
text: If, after nomination of candidacy for the President, Vice- President, member of the Federal Parliament, member of State Assemblies or member of Local level has been filed but before the election results are declared, a question arises about the qualification of a candidate, the Election Commission shall decide that question.

title: Functions, duties and powers of the Election Commission
text: The Election Commission may so delegate any of its functions, duties and powers to the Chief Election Commissioner, Election Commissioner or any government employee as to be exercised and complied with subject to the specified conditions.

title: Functions, duties and powers of the Election Commission
text: Other functions, duties and powers and rules of procedure of the Election Commission shall be as provided for in the Federal law.

title: To provide necessary assistance to Election Commission
text: The Government of Nepal, State Government and Local Government shall provide such employees and other assistance to the Election Commission as may be required to perform its functions pursuant to this Constitution. Part-25 National Human Rights Commission

title: National Human Rights Commission
text: There shall be a National Human Rights Commission of Nepal, consisting of a Chairperson and four other members.

title: National Human Rights Commission
text: The President shall, on the recommendation of the Constitutional Council, appoint the Chairperson and members.

title: National Human Rights Commission
text: The term of office of the Chairperson and members of the National Human Rights Commission shall be six years from the date of appointment.

title: National Human Rights Commission

title: National Human Rights Commission

title: National Human Rights Commission
text: The remuneration and other conditions of service of the Chairperson and members of the National Human Rights Commission shall be as provided for in the Federal law. The remuneration and conditions of service of the Chairperson and members of the National Human Rights Commission shall not, so long as they hold office, be altered to their disadvantage. Provided that this provision shall not apply in cases where a state of emergency is declared because of extreme economic disarrays.

title: National Human Rights Commission
text: A person once appointed as the Chairperson or a member of the National Human Rights Commission shall not be eligible for appointment in other government service. Provided that nothing in this clause shall be deemed to be a bar to the appointment to any political position or to any position which has the responsibility of making investigations, inquiries or findings on any subject, or to any position which has the responsibility of submitting advice, opinion or recommendation after carrying out a study or research on any subject.

title: Functions, duties and powers of National Human Rights Commission
text: It shall be the duty of the National Human Rights Commission to respect, protect and promote human rights and ensure effective enforcement thereof.

title: Functions, duties and powers of National Human Rights Commission
text: For the accomplishment of the duty mentioned in clause

title: Functions, duties and powers of National Human Rights Commission
text: to inquire, on its own initiative or on petition or complaint presented in or sent to the Commission by a victim or any person on his or her behalf or on information received by the Commission from any source, into and investigate complaints of violations of human rights of an individual or group or abetment thereof, and make recommendation for action against the perpetrators,

title: Functions, duties and powers of National Human Rights Commission
text: if any official who has the responsibility or duty to prevent violations of human rights fails to fulfill or perform his or her responsibility or duty or shows reluctance in the fulfillment or performance of his or her responsibility or duty, to make recommendation to the concerned authority to take departmental action against such official,

title: Functions, duties and powers of National Human Rights Commission
text: if it is required to institute a case against any person or organization who has violated human rights, to make recommendation to file a case in the court in accordance with law;

title: Functions, duties and powers of National Human Rights Commission
text: to coordinate and collaborate with the civil society in order to enhance awareness on human rights,

title: Functions, duties and powers of National Human Rights Commission
text: to make recommendation, accompanied by the reasons and grounds, to the concerned body for taking departmental action against and imposing punishment on those who have violated human rights,

title: Functions, duties and powers of National Human Rights Commission
text: to carry out periodic reviews of the relevant laws relating to human rights and make recommendation to the Government of Nepal for necessary improvements in and amendments to such laws,

title: Functions, duties and powers of National Human Rights Commission
text: if Nepal has to become a party to any international treaty or agreement on human rights, to make recommendation, accompanied by the reasons therefor, to the Government of Nepal; and monitor whether any such treaty or agreement to which Nepal is already a party has been implemented, and if it is found not to have been implemented, to make recommendation to the Government of Nepal for its implementation,

title: Functions, duties and powers of National Human Rights Commission
text: to publish, in accordance with law, the names of the officials, persons or bodies who have failed to observe or implement any recommendations or directives made or given by the National Human Rights Commission in relation to the violations of human rights, and record them as violators of human rights.

title: Functions, duties and powers of National Human Rights Commission
text: to exercise all such powers as of a court in respect of the summoning and enforcing the attendance of any person before the Commission and seeking and recording his or her information or statements or depositions, examining evidence and producing exhibits and proofs,

title: Functions, duties and powers of National Human Rights Commission
text: on receipt of information by the Commission in any manner that a serious violation of human rights has already been committed or is going to be committed, to search any person or his or her residence or office, enter such residence or office without notice, and, in the course of making such search, take possession of any document, evidence or proof related with the violation of human rights,

title: Functions, duties and powers of National Human Rights Commission
text: in the event of necessity to take action immediately on receipt of information that the human rights of any person are being violated, to enter any government office or any other place without notice and rescue such person,

title: Functions, duties and powers of National Human Rights Commission
text: to order for the provision of compensation in accordance with law to any person who is a victim of the violations of human rights;

title: Functions, duties and powers of National Human Rights Commission
text: The National Human Rights Commission may so delegate any of its functions, duties and powers to the Chairperson of the Commission, any of its Members or a government employee as to be exercised and complied with subject to the specified conditions.

title: Functions, duties and powers of National Human Rights Commission
text: Other functions, duties and powers and rules of procedure of the National Human Rights Commission shall be as provided for in the Federal law. Part-26 National Natural Resources and Fiscal Commission

title: National Natural Resources and Fiscal Commission
text: There shall be a National Natural Resources and Fiscal Commission of Nepal, consisting of a maximum of five members including a Chairperson.
