title: National Natural Resources and Fiscal Commission
text: having gained expertise upon being active for at least twenty years in the field of natural resources or fiscal management, economics, law, management, after holding a bachelor's degree in the relevant subject from a recognized university,

title: National Natural Resources and Fiscal Commission
text: not being a member of any political party at the time of appointment,

title: National Natural Resources and Fiscal Commission

title: National Natural Resources and Fiscal Commission

text: The remuneration and other conditions of service of the Chairperson and members of the National Natural Resources and Fiscal Commission shall be as provided for in the Federal law, and their remuneration and conditions of service shall not, so long as they hold office, be altered to their disadvantage. Provided that this provision shall not apply in cases where a state of emergency is declared because of extreme economic disarrays.

title: National Natural Resources and Fiscal Commission
text: A person once appointed as the Chairperson or a member of the National Natural Resources and Fiscal Commission shall not be eligible for appointment in other government service. Provided that nothing in this clause shall be deemed to be a bar to the appointment to any political position or to any position which has the responsibility of making investigations, inquiries or findings on any subject, or to any position which has the responsibility of submitting advice, opinion or recommendation after carrying out a study or research on any subject.

title: Functions, duties and powers of National Natural Resources and Fiscal Commission
text: to determine detailed basis and modality for the distribution of revenues between the Federal, State and Local Governments out of the Federal Consolidated Fund in accordance with the Constitution and law,

title: Functions, duties and powers of National Natural Resources and Fiscal Commission
text: to make recommendation about equalization grants to be provided to the State and Local Governments out of the Federal Consolidated Fund,

title: Functions, duties and powers of National Natural Resources and Fiscal Commission
text: to conduct study and research work and prepare parameters as to conditional grants to be provided to the State and Local Governments in accordance with national policies and programs, norms/standards and situation of infrastructures,

title: Functions, duties and powers of National Natural Resources and Fiscal Commission
text: to determine detailed basis and modality for the distribution of revenues between the State and Local Governments out of the State Consolidated Fund,

title: Functions, duties and powers of National Natural Resources and Fiscal Commission
text: to recommend measures to meet expenditures of the Federal, State and Local Governments, and to reform revenue collection,

title: Functions, duties and powers of National Natural Resources and Fiscal Commission
text: to analyze macro-economic indicators and recommend ceiling of internal loans that the Federal, State and Local Governments can borrow,

title: Functions, duties and powers of National Natural Resources and Fiscal Commission
text: to review the bases for the distribution between the Federal and State Governments of revenues and recommend for revision,

title: Functions, duties and powers of National Natural Resources and Fiscal Commission
text: to set bases for the determination of shares of the Government of Nepal, State Government and Local level in investments and returns, in the mobilization of natural resources,

title: Functions, duties and powers of National Natural Resources and Fiscal Commission
text: to do study and research work on possible disputes that may arise between the Federation and the States, between States, between a State and a Local level, and between Local levels, and make suggestions to act in a coordinated manner for the prevention of such disputes.

title: Functions, duties and powers of National Natural Resources and Fiscal Commission
text: The National Natural Resources and Fiscal Commission shall carry out necessary study and research work about environmental impact assessment required in the course of distribution of natural resources, and make recommendations to the Government of Nepal.

title: Functions, duties and powers of National Natural Resources and Fiscal Commission
text: Other functions, duties and powers and rules of procedure of the National Natural Resources and Fiscal Commission, detailed bases required to be followed in the mobilization of natural resources or distribution of revenues, and other matters including conditions of service of the officials of the Commission shall be as provided for in the Federal law. title: Provisions relating to Nepal Army
text: There shall be an organization of the Nepal Army in Nepal, which is inclusive and committed to this Constitution, for the safeguarding of independence, sovereignty, territorial integrity and national unity of Nepal.

title: Provisions relating to Nepal Army
text: The President shall be the supreme commander-in-chief of the Nepal Army.

title: Provisions relating to Nepal Army
text: Entry of women, Dalit, indigenous people, indigenous nationalities, Khas Arya, Madhesi, Tharu, Pichhada class and backward region citizens into the Nepal Army shall, on the basis of principles of equality and inclusion, be ensured by the Federal law.

title: Provisions relating to Nepal Army
text: The Government of Nepal may also mobilize the Nepal Army in other works including development, construction and disaster management works, as provided for in the Federal law.

title: Provisions relating to Nepal Army
text: The President shall, on recommendation of the Council of Ministers, appoint the Commander-in-Chief and remove him or her from office.

title: Provisions relating to Nepal Army
text: The President shall, on recommendation of the National Security Council and pursuant to a decision of the Government of Nepal, Council of Ministers, declare the mobilization of the Nepal Army in cases where a grave emergency arises in regard to the sovereignty or territorial integrity of Nepal or the security of any part thereof, by war, external aggression, armed rebellion or extreme economic disarray. A declaration of the mobilization of the Nepal Army must be ratified by the House of Representatives within one month after the date of such declaration.

title: Provisions relating to Nepal Army
text: Other matters pertaining to the Nepal Army shall be in accordance with law.

title: Provisions Relating to Nepal Police, Armed Police Force, Nepal and National Investigation Department
text: The Federation shall have Nepal Police, Armed Police Force, Nepal and National Investigation Department.

title: Provisions Relating to Nepal Police, Armed Police Force, Nepal and National Investigation Department

title: Provisions Relating to Nepal Police, Armed Police Force, Nepal and National Investigation Department

title: Formation, registration and operation of political parties
text: If the name, objective, insignia or flag of a political party is of such a nature as to jeopardize the religious and communal unity of the country or to fragment the country, that party shall not be registered.

title: Prohibition on imposition of restrictions on political parties
text: Any law, arrangement or decision so made as to impose any restriction on the formation and operation of a political party and on the generation of publicity in order to secure support and cooperation from the general public for the ideology, philosophy and program of the party in accordance with Article 269 shall be deemed to be inconsistent with this Constitution and shall, ipso facto, be void.

title: Prohibition on imposition of restrictions on political parties
text: Any law, arrangement or decision so made as to allow for participation or involvement of only a single political party or persons having similar political ideology, philosophy or program in the elections or in the political system of, or in the conduct of governance of, the country shall be deemed to be inconsistent with this Constitution and shall, ipso facto, be void.

title: Registration required for securing recognition for the purpose of contesting elections as political party
text: Every political party that is registered in accordance with Article 269 and intends to secure recognition from the Election Commission for the purposes of elections must be registered with the Election Commission in fulfilment of the procedures set forth in the Federal law.

title: Registration required for securing recognition for the purpose of contesting elections as political party

title: Registration required for securing recognition for the purpose of contesting elections as political party

title: Other provisions relating to political parties
text: Provisions relating to the formation, registration, operation and facilities of political parties and other matters pertaining thereto shall be as provided for in the Federal law. Part-30 Emergency Power

title: Emergency power
text: If a grave emergency arises in regard to the sovereignty, territorial integrity of Nepal or the security of any part thereof, by war, external aggression, armed rebellion, extreme economic disarray, natural calamity or epidemic, the President may declare or order a state of emergency in respect of the whole of Nepal or of any specified part thereof.

title: Emergency power
text: Notwithstanding anything contained in clause

title: Emergency power
text: , if there arises a grave emergency in a State by a natural calamity or epidemic, the concerned State Government may request the Government of Nepal to declare or order a state of emergency in respect of the whole of the State or of any specified part thereof, in accordance with this Article.

title: Emergency power
text: Every declaration or order made in accordance with clause

title: Emergency power
text: shall be laid before both Houses of the Federal Parliament for approval within one month after the date of issuance of such proclamation or order.

title: Emergency power

title: Emergency power
text: is approved by at least two-thirds majority of the total number of the then members of both Houses of the Federal Parliament, the declaration or order shall continue in force for a period of three months from the date of its issuance.

title: Emergency power

title: Emergency power

title: Emergency power

title: Emergency power

title: Emergency power
text: If, during the continuance of a declaration or order under this Article, any injury is caused to a person from any act done by any official in bad faith, the victim may, within three months from the date of termination of that declaration or order, file a petition for compensation for such injury. If such petition is made, the court may order for compensation by, and punish, the perpetrator as provided for in the Federal law.

title: Emergency power
text: The President may, at any time, withdraw a declaration or order of a state of emergency made in accordance with this Article. Part-31 Amendment to the Constitution

title: Amendment to Constitution
text: No amendment shall be made to this Constitution in manner to be prejudicial to the sovereignty, territorial integrity, independence of Nepal and sovereignty vested in the people.

title: Amendment to Constitution
text: Subject to clause

title: Amendment to Constitution
text: and other Articles of this Constitution, a Bill to amend or repeal any Article of this Constitution may be introduced in either House of the Federal Parliament. Provided that clause

title: Amendment to Constitution
text: shall not be amended.

title: Amendment to Constitution
text: A Bill introduced pursuant to clause

title: Amendment to Constitution
text: shall be publicly published for information to the general public within thirty days of its introduction in the concerned House.

title: Amendment to Constitution
text: If a Bill introduced pursuant to clause

title: Amendment to Constitution
text: is related with the alteration in the borders of any State or matters set forth in Schedule-6, the Speaker or the Chairperson of the concerned House must send that Bill to the State Assembly for its consent, within thirty days after its introduction in the Federal Parliament.

title: Amendment to Constitution
text: The concerned State Assembly must, by a majority of the total number of its the then members, accept or reject the Bill presented for its consent pursuant to clause

title: Amendment to Constitution
text: and give information thereof to the Federal Parliament within three months. Provided that if any State Assembly is not in existence, such Bill must be either accepted or rejected within three months after the date of holding of the first meeting following the formation of that State Assembly.

title: Amendment to Constitution
text: In the event of failure to give information of acceptance or rejection of such Bill within the period set forth in clause

title: Amendment to Constitution
text: , nothing shall prevent the House of the Federal Parliament in which the Bill was originated from proceeding with the Bill.

title: Amendment to Constitution
text: If the State Assembly, by its majority, gives a notice of rejection of such Bill to the concerned House of the Federal Parliament within the period under clause

title: Amendment to Constitution
text: , the Bill shall be inoperative.

title: Amendment to Constitution
text: A Bill which does not require the consent of the State Assembly or a Bill accepted by a majority of the State Assembly under clause

title: Amendment to Constitution
text: must be passed by at least two-thirds majority of the total number of the then members of both Houses of the Federal Parliament.

title: Amendment to Constitution
text: A Bill passed under clause

title: Amendment to Constitution
text: shall be presented to the President for assent.

title: Amendment to Constitution
text: The President shall give assent to a Bill presented under clause

title: Amendment to Constitution
text: , within fifteen days from the date of its receipt, and the Constitution shall get amended from the date of assent. Part-32 Miscellaneous

title: Provisions relating to referendum
text: If a decision is made by a two-thirds majority of the total number of the then members of the Federal Parliament that it is necessary to hold a referendum with respect to any matter of national importance, decision on that matter may be taken by way of referendum.

title: Provisions relating to referendum
text: Matters relating to referendum and other relevant matters shall be as provided for in the Federal law.

title: Pardons
text: The President may, in accordance with law, grant pardons, suspend, commute or remit any sentence passed by any Court, judicial or quasi-judicial body or administrative authority or body.

title: Titles, honours and decorations
text: The President shall confer titles, honours and decorations to be conferred on behalf of the State.

title: Titles, honours and decorations
text: No citizen of Nepal may, without the approval of the Government of Nepal, accept any title, honour or decoration from any foreign government.

title: Power to make treaties
text: The Federation shall have the power to make treaties or agreements.

title: Power to make treaties
text: In making a treaty or agreement on a matter falling within the list of State power, the Government of Nepal must consult the concerned State.

title: Power to make treaties
text: A State Council of Ministers may, with the consent of the Government of Nepal, make contractual agreements on financial and industrial matters.

title: Ratification of, accession to, acceptance of, or approval of, treaties or agreements
text: The ratification of, accession to, acceptance of, or approval of, treaties or agreements to which Nepal or the Government of Nepal is to become a party shall be as provided for in the Federal law.

title: Ratification of, accession to, acceptance of, or approval of, treaties or agreements

title: Ratification of, accession to, acceptance of, or approval of, treaties or agreements
text: and

title: Ratification of, accession to, acceptance of, or approval of, treaties or agreements
text: , if any treaty or agreement is of an ordinary nature, which does not affect the nation extensively, seriously or in the long term, the ratification of, accession to, acceptance of, or approval of, such treaty or agreement may be made by a simple majority of the members present in a meeting of the House of Representatives.

title: Ratification of, accession to, acceptance of, or approval of, treaties or agreements
text: After the commencement of this Constitution, unless a treaty or agreement is ratified, acceded to, accepted or approved in accordance with this Article, such treaty or agreement shall not apply to the Government of Nepal or Nepal.

title: Ratification of, accession to, acceptance of, or approval of, treaties or agreements

title: Special provision relating to discharge of functions of President
text: If the offices of both the President and the Vice-President fall vacant in accordance with this Constitution, the Speaker of the House of Representatives shall discharge the functions required to be performed by the President under this Constitution until election to the President or Vice-President is held and he or she assumes office.

title: Appraisal and review of special rights
text: The Government of Nepal shall make appraisal and review of the implementation of special rights of the women and Dalit community and impacts thereof, on the basis of human development index, concurrently with a national census to be held in every ten years.

title: Ambassadors and special emissaries
text: The President may, on the basis of the principle of inclusion, appoint Nepalese ambassadors, and special emissaries for any specific purposes.

title: Ambassadors and special emissaries
text: The President shall receive letters of credentials from foreign ambassadors and diplomatic representatives.

title: Appointments to be made in accordance with inclusive principle
text: Appointments to offices of Constitutional Organs and Bodies shall be made in accordance with the inclusive principle.

title: Provisions relating to Constitutional Council
text: The Prime Minister -Chairperson

title: Provisions relating to Constitutional Council
text: While making a recommendation for appointment to the office of the Chief Justice, the Constitutional Council shall include the Minister for Law and Justice of the Government of Nepal, as its member.

title: Provisions relating to Constitutional Council
text: The Constitutional Council must make a recommendation for appointment under this Constitution before one month of the vacation of the office of the Chief Justice or a chief or official of a Constitutional Body. Provided that if such office falls vacant because of death or resignation, it may so make recommendation for appointment that the office is fulfilled within one month after the date of vacancy.

title: Provisions relating to Constitutional Council
text: Other functions, duties and powers of the Constitutional Council and rules of procedures on the appointment of the Chief Justice or chiefs or officials of Constitutional Bodies shall be as provided for in the Federal law.

title: Provisions relating to Constitutional Council
text: The Chief Secretary of the Government of Nepal shall act as the secretary of the Constitutional Council.

title: Constitution of government service
text: The Government of Nepal may, in order to run the administration of the country, constitute the Federal civil service and such other Federal government services as may be required. The constitution, operation and conditions of service of such services shall be as provided for in the Federal Act.

title: Constitution of government service
text: Positions in the Federal civil service as well as all Federal government services shall be filled through competitive examinations, on the basis of open and proportional inclusive principle.

title: Constitution of government service
text: The State Council of Ministers, Village Executives and Municipal Executives may by law constitute and operate various government services as required for the operation of their administration.

title: Election Constituency Delimitation Commission
text: While determining election constituencies pursuant to this Article, the Election Constituency Delimitation Commission shall so determine the constituencies, having regard to population and geography as the basis of representation, and as to ensure that the ratio between the geography, population and number of members of such election constituencies is equal, so far as practicable.

title: Election Constituency Delimitation Commission
text: While delimitating election constituencies pursuant to clause

title: Election Constituency Delimitation Commission
text: , regard must be had to, inter alia, the density of population, geographical specificity, administrative and transportation convenience, community and cultural aspects of the constituencies.

title: Election Constituency Delimitation Commission
text: No question may be raised in any court on any matter of the determination, or review, of election constituencies made by the Election Constituency Delimitation Commission.

title: Election Constituency Delimitation Commission
text: The Election Constituency Delimitation Commission shall submit a report on the works it has performed to the Government of Nepal.

title: Election Constituency Delimitation Commission

title: Election Constituency Delimitation Commission
text: before the Federal Parliament and send it to the Election Commission for implementation.

title: Election Constituency Delimitation Commission
text: The Election Constituency Delimitation Commission shall determine its rules of procedures itself.

title: Election Constituency Delimitation Commission
text: The remuneration and facilities of the Chairperson and the members of the Election Constituency Delimitation Commission shall be similar to those of the Chief Election Commissioner and the Election Commissioners of the Election Commission, respectively.

title: Election Constituency Delimitation Commission
text: The election constituencies delimited in accordance with clause

title: Election Constituency Delimitation Commission
text: must be reviewed in every twenty years.

title: Election Constituency Delimitation Commission
text: The Government of Nepal shall provide such employees as may be required for the Election Constituency Delimitation Commission.

title: Language Commission
text: The Government of Nepal shall, no later than one year of the commencement of this Constitution, constitute a Language Commission comprising representation of States.

title: Language Commission
text: The Language Commission shall consist of one Chairperson and a required number of members.

title: Language Commission
text: The term of office of the Chairperson and members of the Language Commission shall be six years from the date of their appointment. They may not be reappointed.

title: Language Commission

title: Capital
text: The capital of Nepal shall be situated in Kathmandu.

title: Capital
text: The capital of a State under this Constitution shall be as decided by a two-thirds majority of the number of the then members of the concerned State Assembly.

title: Capital

title: Special provision relating to citizenship of officials
text: In order for a person to be elected, nominated or appointed to the office of President, Vice-President, Prime Minister, Chief Justice, Speaker of the House of Representatives, Chief of State, Chief Minister, Speaker of a State Assembly and chief of a security body, the person must have obtained the citizenship of Nepal by descent.

title: Special provision relating to citizenship of officials
text: A person who has obtained the citizenship of Nepal by descent, a person who has obtained the naturalized citizenship of Nepal or a person who has obtained the citizenship of Nepal by birth shall also be qualified for the office of a constitutional body other than that mentioned in clause

title: Special provision relating to citizenship of officials
text: . Provided that the person must have resided in Nepal for at least ten years in the case of a person who has obtained the naturalized citizenship of Nepal, and for at least five years in the case of a person who has obtained the citizenship of Nepal by birth or who has obtained the naturalized citizenship of Nepal in accordance with clause

title: Special provision relating to citizenship of officials
text: of Article 11.

title: Provisions relating to Guthi (trusts)
text: The Federal Parliament shall make necessary laws in relation to the rights of the trust and the farmers enjoying possessory rights over trust lands in a manner not to be prejudicial to the basic norms of the trusts.

title: Provisions relating to Guthi (trusts)
text: Other matters relating to trusts shall be as provided by the federal law.

title: Not to be qualified for appointment
text: Notwithstanding anything contained elsewhere in this Constitution, a citizen of Nepal who has obtained a foreign permanent residence permit shall not be qualified for election, nomination or appointment to an office to be elected, nominated or appointed pursuant to this Constitution. Provided that nothing shall prevent electing, nominating or appointing a person who has renounced such foreign permanent residence permit to such office after the expiry of a period of at least three months.

title: Not to be qualified for appointment
text: Other matters relating to the citizens of Nepal who have obtained foreign permanent residence permits as mentioned in clause

title: Not to be qualified for appointment
text: shall be as provided for in the Federal law.

title: Provisions relating to parliamentary hearings
text: Parliamentary hearings shall be conducted as to appointments to the offices of the Chief Justice and Judges of the Supreme Court, members of the Judicial Council, chiefs and members of Constitutional Bodies, who are appointed on the recommendation of the Constitutional Council under this Constitution, and to the offices of ambassadors, as provided for in the Federal law.

title: Provisions relating to parliamentary hearings
text: For the purposes of clause

title: Provisions relating to parliamentary hearings
text: , there shall be formed a fifteen- member joint committee consisting of members of both Houses of the Federal Parliament, in accordance with the Federal law.

title: Provisions relating to parliamentary hearings
text: No member of the joint committee under clause

title: Provisions relating to parliamentary hearings
text: shall practice law in the Supreme Court during that term of the Federal Parliament.

title: Monitoring of functioning of Constitutional Bodies
text: The chiefs and officials of the Constitutional Bodies must be accountable and responsible to the Federal Parliament. The committees of the House of Representatives may monitor and evaluate the functioning, including reports, of the Constitutional Bodies, other than the National Human Rights Commission, and give necessary direction or advice.

title: Annual reports of Constitutional Bodies
text: Every Constitutional Body under this Constitution shall submit an annual report of its functioning to the President, and the President shall cause that report to be laid through the Prime Minister before the Federal Parliament.

title: Annual reports of Constitutional Bodies
text: The matters to be set out in the annual report under clause

title: Annual reports of Constitutional Bodies

title: Annual reports of Constitutional Bodies

title: Annual reports of Constitutional Bodies
text: , a Constitutional Body may prepare a separate report in relation to the functioning of each State and submit it to the Chief of State. Part-33 Transitional Provisions

title: Constitution of Federal Commission
text: The Government of Nepal may constitute a Federal Commission for making suggestions on matters relating to the boundaries of States.

title: Constitution of Federal Commission

title: Constitution of Federal Commission

title: Constitution of Federal Commission
text: shall be constituted no later than six months of the date of commencement of this Constitution. Its term shall be one year.

title: Constituent Assembly to be converted into Legislature-Parliament
text: The Constituent Assembly existing at the time of commencement of this Constitution shall ipso facto be converted into the Legislature-Parliament after the commencement of this Constitution, and the term of such Legislature- Parliament shall exist until 7 Magha 2074. Provided that if an election is held to the House of Representatives set forth in this Constitution prior to the expiration of that term, the Legislature- Parliament shall continue to exist until the day before the day specified for the filing of nominations of candidates for that election.

title: Constituent Assembly to be converted into Legislature-Parliament

title: Constituent Assembly to be converted into Legislature-Parliament
text: The legislative power of the State Assembly with respect of matters set forth in Schedule-6 shall, upon the commencement of this Constitution, be vested in the Legislature-Parliament set forth in clause

title: Constituent Assembly to be converted into Legislature-Parliament

title: Constituent Assembly to be converted into Legislature-Parliament
text: The Legislature-Parliament Secretariat, its Secretary General, Secretary and employees existing at the time of commencement of this Constitution shall, subject to the conditions of service in force at the time of their appointment, exist in the Federal Parliament Secretariat set forth in this Constitution.

title: Constituent Assembly to be converted into Legislature-Parliament
text: Where the Legislature-Parliament is in recess at the time of commencement of this Constitution, the President shall summon its session no later than seven days after the date of commencement of this Constitution. The President shall thereafter summon a session of the Legislature-Parliament from time to time.

title: Provisions relating to the President and the Vice-President
text: The President and the Vice-President existing at the time of commencement of this Constitution shall continue to hold their respective offices until other President and Vice-President are elected in accordance with this Article.

text: The composition and allocation of portfolios of the Council of Ministers to be formed in accordance with this Article shall be made through mutual understanding.

title: Provisions relating to formation of Council of Ministers
text: The Council of Ministers to be formed in accordance with this Article shall consist of a Deputy Prime Minister and other Ministers, as required.

title: Provisions relating to formation of Council of Ministers
text: The Prime Minister appointed in accordance with this Article shall, in appointing Ministers in accordance with clause

title: Provisions relating to formation of Council of Ministers
text: , appoint Ministers from amongst the members of the Legislature-Parliament, on the recommendation of the concerned party.

title: Provisions relating to formation of Council of Ministers
text: The Prime Minister and other Ministers appointed in accordance with this Article shall be collectively responsible to the Legislature-Parliament, and the Ministers shall be individually responsible to the Prime Minister and the Legislature- Parliament for the work of their respective Ministries.

title: Provisions relating to formation of Council of Ministers
text: if he or she tenders resignation in writing to the President,

title: Provisions relating to formation of Council of Ministers
text: if a vote of no-confidence is passed against him or her or a vote of confidence is not passed in accordance with clause

title: Provisions relating to formation of Council of Ministers
text: if he or she ceases to be a member of the Legislature-Parliament,

title: Provisions relating to formation of Council of Ministers
text: if he or she dies.

title: Provisions relating to formation of Council of Ministers
text: if he or she tenders resignation in writing to the Prime Minister,

title: Provisions relating to formation of Council of Ministers
text: if the Prime Minister is removed from office in accordance with clause

title: Provisions relating to formation of Council of Ministers
text: if he or she is removed from office by the Prime Minister on the recommendation or advice of the concerned party,

title: Provisions relating to formation of Council of Ministers
text: if he or she dies.

title: Provisions relating to formation of Council of Ministers
text: Even though the office of the Prime Minister falls vacant under clause

title: Provisions relating to formation of Council of Ministers
text: , the same Council of Ministers shall continue to act until another Council of Ministers is constituted.

title: Provisions relating to formation of Council of Ministers
text: If the Prime Minister appointed in accordance with this Article dies, the Deputy Prime Minister or senior-most Minister shall act as the Prime Minister until another Prime Minister is appointed.

title: Provisions relating to formation of Council of Ministers
text: The Prime Minister appointed in accordance with this Article may, whenever he or she considers necessary or appropriate to show/clarify that he or she has confidence from the Legislature-Parliament, table a resolution to that effect in the Legislature-Parliament for the vote of confidence.

title: Provisions relating to formation of Council of Ministers
text: At least one-fourth of the total number of the then members of the Legislature-Parliament may table in writing a motion of no-confidence against the Prime Minister appointed in accordance with this Article. Provided that a motion of no confidence shall not be tabled more than once in six moths' period against the same Prime Minister appointed in accordance with this Article.

title: Provisions relating to formation of Council of Ministers
text: A motion tabled under clauses

title: Provisions relating to formation of Council of Ministers
text: and

title: Provisions relating to formation of Council of Ministers
text: shall be decided by a majority of the total number of the then members of the Legislature Parliament.

title: Provisions relating to formation of Council of Ministers
text: The Government of Nepal shall, after the commencement of this Constitution, exercise the powers of a State until the Council of Ministers of the State set forth in this Constitution is formed.

title: Provisions relating to Speaker and Deputy Speaker
text: The Speaker and Deputy Speaker existing at the time of commencement of this Constitution shall continue to hold their respective offices until another Speaker and Deputy Speaker is elected pursuant to this Constitution.

title: Provisions relating to Speaker and Deputy Speaker
text: The members of the Legislature-Parliament shall, on the basis of political understanding, elect one Speaker and one Deputy Speaker from themselves no later than twenty days after the date of the commencement of this Constitution where the Legislature-Parliament is not in recess at the time of commencement of this Constitution, and after the date on which the session of the Legislature-Parliament is summoned in accordance with clause

title: Provisions relating to Speaker and Deputy Speaker
text: of Article 296 where the Legislature-Parliament is in recess.

title: Provisions relating to Speaker and Deputy Speaker
text: In the event of failure to have an understanding under clause

title: Provisions relating to Speaker and Deputy Speaker
text: , a member of the Legislature-Parliament who commands a majority of all the then members of the Legislature-Parliament shall be deemed to be elected as the Speaker or Deputy Speaker.

title: Provisions relating to Speaker and Deputy Speaker
text: In holding electing in accordance with clause

title: Provisions relating to Speaker and Deputy Speaker
text: or

title: Provisions relating to Speaker and Deputy Speaker
text: , the Speaker and the Deputy Speaker shall be members representing different political parties in the Legislature-Parliament.

title: Provisions relating to Speaker and Deputy Speaker
text: In discharging his or her functions in accordance with this Constitution, the Speaker or Deputy Speaker shall so discharge functions in the capacity of a neutral person without standing for or against any political party whatsoever.

title: Provisions relating to Speaker and Deputy Speaker
text: if he or she resigns in writing,

title: Provisions relating to Speaker and Deputy Speaker
text: if he or she ceases to be a member of the Legislature-Parliament,

title: Provisions relating to Speaker and Deputy Speaker
text: if a resolution is passed by a majority of at least two-thirds of the total number of the members of the Legislature-Parliament to the effect that his or her conduct is not compatible with his or her office,

title: Provisions relating to Speaker and Deputy Speaker
text: if he or she dies.

title: Provisions relating to Speaker and Deputy Speaker
text: The Deputy Speaker or another member shall preside over a meeting at which deliberations are to be held on a resolution that the conduct of the Speaker of the Legislature-Parliament is not compatible with his or her office, and the Speaker may take part and vote in the deliberations on such resolution.

title: Provisions relating to Speaker and Deputy Speaker
text: Other procedures on the election to the Speaker and Deputy Speaker and procedures on the moving and passage of a motion that the Speaker or Deputy Speaker has committed conduct not compatible with his or her office shall be as provided by the then prevailing rules of the Legislature- Parliament.

title: Provisions relating to Judiciary
text: The Supreme Court, Constituent Assembly Court, Appellate Courts and District Courts existing at the time of commencement of this Constitution shall continue to exist until the structure of Judiciary set forth in this Constitution is set up. Nothing in this Constitution shall be deemed to bar the settlement by respective Courts of the cases already filed in such Courts prior to the commencement of this Constitution and of the cases to be filed after the commencement of this Constitution.

title: Provisions relating to Judiciary
text: The Chief Justice and Judges of the Supreme Court, Chief Judges and Judges of the Appellate Courts and Judges of the Districts Counts shall be deemed to have been appointed under this Constitution.

title: Provisions relating to Judiciary
text: The High Courts set forth in Article 139 shall be established no later than one year after the date of commencement of this Constitution. The Appellate Courts existing at the time of commencement of this Constitution shall be dissolved after the establishment of such Courts.

title: Provisions relating to Judiciary
text: After the establishment of the High Courts in accordance with clause

title: Provisions relating to Judiciary
text: , the cases sub judice in the Appellate Courts shall be transferred to such High Courts as specified by the Government of Nepal, in consultation with the Judicial Council, by notification in the Nepal Gazette.

title: Provisions relating to Judiciary
text: After the establishment of the High Courts in accordance with clause

title: Provisions relating to Judiciary
text: , the Chief Justice shall, on recommendation of the Judicial Council, post the Chief Judges and Judges of the Appellate Courts who are holding office at the time of commencement of this Constitution to the offices of the Chief Judges and Judges of the High Courts.

title: Provisions relating to Judiciary
text: The Additional Judges of the Appellate Courts who are holding office at the time of commencement of this Constitution may continue to hold office until the term specified at the time of their appointment.

title: Provisions relating to Judiciary
text: The cases on criminal offences punishable by imprisonment for a term of more than one year and sub judice in any body other than a Court at the time of commencement of this Constitution shall, after the commencement of this Constitution, be transferred to the concerned District Court.

title: Provisions relating to Constitutional Bodies and officials thereof
text: The Constitutional Bodies which are existing at the time of commencement of this Constitution and provided in this Constitution shall be deemed to have been formed under this Constitution, and nothing shall be deemed to bar the settlement of the matters under consideration of such Bodies subject to this Constitution.

title: Provisions relating to Constitutional Bodies and officials thereof
text: The chiefs or officials of the Constitutional Bodies holding office at the time of commencement of this Constitution shall be deemed to have been appointed in accordance with this Constitution and shall continue to hold their respective offices subject to the conditions of services at the time of their appointments.

title: Provisions relating to Constitutional Bodies and officials thereof
text: The officials holding office in the Commission for the Investigation of the Abuse of Authority and the Public Service Commission, at the time of commencement of this Constitution, in excess of the number specified in this Constitution, shall continue to hold their respective offices subject to the conditions of services at the time of their appointments.

title: Formation and operation of government services at State and Local levels
text: The Government of Nepal shall make necessary provisions for the delivery of services at the State and Local levels.

title: Formation and operation of government services at State and Local levels
text: The Government of Nepal may, in making provisions under clause

title: Formation and operation of government services at State and Local levels
text: , arrange for the delivery of services by making adjustment of the employees serving in the government services at the time of commencement of this Constitution with the Federal, State and Local levels in accordance with law.

title: Provisions relating to Local Bodies
text: The Local Bodies existing at the time of Commencement of this Constitution shall continue to exist until the determination of the number and areas of the Local level in accordance with this Constitution.

title: Provisions relating to Local Bodies
text: Election to the officials of the Local Bodies existing under clause

title: Provisions relating to Local Bodies
text: shall be held in accordance with law.

title: Provisions relating to Local Bodies
text: The officials of the Local Bodies elected in accordance with clause

title: Provisions relating to Local Bodies
text: shall continue to hold office until election to the Local level is held in accordance with this Constitution.

title: Existing laws to remain in force
text: The Nepal laws in force at the time of commencement of this Constitution shall continue to be in force until such laws are repealed or amended. Provided that any law which is inconsistent with this Constitution shall ipso facto be invalid to the extent of such inconsistency, after one year of the date on which the first session of the Federal Parliament set forth in this Constitution is held.

title: Existing laws to remain in force
text: Acts relating to peace process under the Interim Constitution of Nepal, 2007 (2063) shall be deemed to have been done under this Constitution.

title: Power to remove difficulties
text: If any difficulty arises in connection with the implementation of this Constitution until the commencement of the first session of the Federal Parliament, upon being elected in accordance with this Constitution, the President may, on the recommendation of the Government of Nepal, Council of Ministers, issue necessary orders to remove such difficulty; and such orders must be submitted to the Legislature-Parliament or Federal Parliament, held immediately after the issue such orders, for approval. Part-34 Definitions and Interpretations

title: Definitions and interpretations
text: "minorities" means ethnic, linguistic and religious groups whose population is less than the percentage specified by the Federal law, and includes groups that have their distinct ethnic, religious or linguistic characteristics, aspirations to protect such features and subjected to discrimination and oppression,

title: Definitions and interpretations
text: "law" means a Federal law, State law and Local law,

title: Definitions and interpretations
text: "Article" means an article of this Constitution,

title: Definitions and interpretations
text: "Municipality" means and includes a Municipal Corporation and Sub-municipal Corporation.

title: Definitions and interpretations
text: "citizen" means a citizen of Nepal,

title: Definitions and interpretations
text: "State" means the area and form of a federal unit of Nepal divided into the Federal units in accordance with this Constitution,

title: Definitions and interpretations
text: "remuneration" means and includes salary, allowance, pension and any other form of emolument and facility,

title: Definitions and interpretations
text: "state power" means the power relating to the Executive, Legislative and Judiciary of the state, and includes residual power.

title: Definitions and interpretations
text: "Bill" means a draft of amendment to the Constitution or of an Act which has been introduced in the Federal Parliament or a State Assembly,

title: Definitions and interpretations
text: "Federation" means the federal level that is the apex unit of the federal structure,

title: Definitions and interpretations
text: "Federal Units" means the Federation, State and Local levels,

title: Definitions and interpretations
text: "Constitutional Bodies" means the Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority, Auditor General, Public Service Commission, Election Commission, National Human Rights Commission, National Natural Resources and Fiscal Commission, National Women Commission, National Dalit Commission, National Inclusion Commission, Indigenous Nationalities Commission, Madhesi Commission, Tharu Commission and Muslim Commission,

title: Definitions and interpretations
text: "marginalized" means communities that are made politically, economically and socially backward, are unable to enjoy services and facilities because of discrimination and oppression and of geographical remoteness or deprived thereof and are in lower status than the human development standards mentioned in Federal law, and includes highly marginalized groups and groups on the verge of extinction,

title: Definitions and interpretations
text: "Local level" means the Village Bodies, Municipalities and District Assemblies to be established in accordance with this Constitution.

title: Definitions and interpretations
text: Unless the subject or the context otherwise requires, the legal provisions relating to interpretation of laws shall, subject to the provisions of this Constitution, apply to the interpretation of this Constitution in the same manner as that provisions apply to the interpretation of the Nepal laws. Part-35 Short Title, Commencement and Repeal

title: Short title and commencement
text: This Constitution may be cited as the "Constitution of Nepal".

title: Short title and commencement
text: This Constitution shall commence on 20 September 2015 (3rd day of the month of Ashwin of the year 2072 Bikram Sambat).

title: Repeal
text: The Interim Constitution of Nepal, 2007 (2063) is hereby repealed.
